reviews.review_text:
Really thought I could like this game, but couldn't get past even the basic parts of them. Coming from a background of steep learning curve games, this one didn't feel like I wanted to invest any time in, because I had lookup multiple things that should be obvious in a game like this. Basically, the user experience is not intuitive, so you end up searching for things that you shouldn't be searching for and should have been easily discovered in the game. 

Plus the latency is quite bad in multiplayer, if you're living far from your peers. Most games handle this more elegantly, where I believe at least basic movement is local and synced with teh server, but just moving around feels like rubberband.

One annoying practice is also the bloating of all the available items in the game including >50% which are behind paywall, and even worse I can't even hide those. It's like the game is saying "yeah you just paid for base game, you only get the demo".  I don't mind some paid unlocks/DLCs but they shouldn't feel "in you face" all the time, feels like a Paradox game.

this game is fun, but i tell you all new players. please take your time build and dont rush your builds and if you do not know how to make things work, well just dont worry about that first, just build the shell first like in away you do in minecraft, then add the important stuff later, after you get done with what you would wanted, then add power or the core to give your ship power so you no your ship has power, the lights will come online. also never start Survival mode first start creative mode to laren how to make things work well you build and just play with what you make and just test things out how you want it, to work. dont be scared of trying things out, you could say your builds and save them as blueprint's or save world, if you f up or you save by accident or have auto save on, just go to your world and go to back up save's then click any back up save's and click the save file and you will load in your world on that last save... okay now for mods dont worry about that yet until you get a hand of the game just baby step's mods are nice but not the important than learning how to make your firt build's also in the settings when making a new world there are options somthing like minecraft dose,  when you get there turn off damage blocks as off for now just to get hang of the game, for real so you could have better experience also, comes to bit more advace theres some youtube videos will show you on how to do thing's or ask the community for help also there is SE discord its most likely get help there.. its the best i could explan it to new players

Been playing this game for a long time and this is one of the truly limitless sandboxes. ive pushed this game beyond what its built to do and have yet to find a limit.

If you want something you can truly explore and feel progress playing space engineers is it.

Space Engineers is basically what happens when somebody gives engineers unlimited freedom and says, “Yeah sure, go build whatever you want in space,” without considering the consequences.

What I love most about the game is the pure creativity it allows. You can build almost anything — small ships, massive cruisers, mining stations, factories, planetary bases, walkers, ridiculous death machines, or flying bricks held together entirely by optimism and questionable engineering decisions.

The building system is honestly one of the most satisfying I’ve used in a sandbox game. Watching a ship slowly come together block by block and then actually function feels incredibly rewarding. And when it doesn’t function, which absolutely happens, the physics engine usually turns your mistake into a spectacular disaster.

The game also does a great job making engineering actually matter. Power management, fuel, weight distribution, conveyor systems, oxygen, gravity, thrust direction — everything plays a role. Half the gameplay becomes solving problems you accidentally created yourself.

I also really enjoy the survival aspect. Starting with almost nothing and slowly building up a functioning base or ship makes progression feel earned. Mining resources, setting up refineries, automating production, and expanding operations scratches the same part of my brain that games like Factorio or Satisfactory do.

And then there’s space itself, which the game nails surprisingly well. Flying through asteroid fields, landing on planets, building orbital stations, or accidentally drifting into space because you forgot to add enough thrusters creates some genuinely memorable moments.

Multiplayer is where the game becomes absolute chaos in the best way possible. Every server eventually turns into a mix of brilliant engineering, accidental explosions, pirate attacks, and somebody asking why the station is suddenly rotating.

What really keeps me playing though is how every project feels personal. You’re not just unlocking pre-made vehicles — you’re designing your own solutions, and that makes every successful ship or base feel rewarding in a different way than most survival games.

That said, the game definitely has a learning curve. The interface and systems can feel overwhelming at first, and physics sometimes decide reality is optional. Performance can also become questionable once players start building massive creations that probably violate several laws of engineering and common sense.

But honestly, that unpredictability is also part of the charm for me. Some of my favorite moments came from complete mechanical failures turning into accidental comedy.

For me, Space Engineers is one of the best sandbox building games out there because it gives players an absurd amount of freedom while still making engineering and problem-solving feel meaningful. It’s creative, complex, occasionally terrifying, and incredibly satisfying when everything finally works the way you intended.
I keep coming back because it scratches that itch. Definitely worth playing especially if you've got friends to play with. Space Engineers 2 is going to be great!

This game is so beginner-unfriendly it's insane. I wanted to try this game with a friend, but little did I know it would be a nightmare to even figure out how to do that. You can't do the tutorials maps OR quick start with a friend. You HAVE to create a new game, and you're presented with so many options you don't even have a clue which one is the right one to pick. Then you get into the game, and you have no idea how to even find your friend. I literally had to go to the internet to figure it out because this game is so convoluted. Ended up spending nearly 3 hours not even playing the game, just trying to figure out how things work and trying to find my friend on the map.

I bet this game can be fun, but only after you've taken an entire educational course on how to play it. Honestly can't believe I wasted my money on this garbage game that doesn't care about or even understand a new player.

wtf is it with these space building games and there lack of proper in-game tutorials, how many hours would you like me to run around aimlessly? i dont get it its either a poorly put together tutorial or some tiring to read codex.
Not for me, a Mistake can wipe out 20 hours of progress, and respawn you're having a laugh 50 miles from base, there is nothing relaxing about this game.
literally have not been able to start the other than once when i restart my pc and now that doesnt even work

This game has huge potential and was clearly made by people that like engineering. That's where my praise stops however. It's so unbelievably buggy that I am stopping playing this. I made the simplest ship imaginable , it flew very well, when docked with the base it just stops working competely, thrusters wouldnt fire, even though it had tonnes of fuel, and when i dock the hydrogen is syphoned into the abyss and lost forever it seems even though nothing is set to stock pile. Spent 3 hours trouble shooting it with not luck and released, damn, this isnt fun at all.
I tried to play this game. It looks cool, but I keep experiencing crashes and lag. I have a high-end PC, so that's definitely not the issue. Yeah, don't buy it.

KEEEEN!
Yes very cute DLC blocks, now fix the game!

I have been playing this for few thousand hours now and I have tried everything: Attach hinge head to rotor, attach wheel to hinge, use merge blocks to create the most unholy creations ever and had so much fun doing so!
However, Keen has in it's inifite wisdom decided to make the game mechanics "safer" (read: BOOORING!!!), by constanly denying certain ways of build one step at a time.

Also latest addition is to make subgrids static without ability to convert them back to dynamic!!! MAN WTF!! You drunk or what??

GIVE ME BACK THE ABILITY TO DO INSANE CREATIONS! I TAKE FULL RESPONSIBILITY OF CLANG THAT WILL OCCUR!!

Game slogan should be: "Need to create.. but not like that.. or that. You can't do that either! Only the way we allow it!"

and you have deliberately blocked attaching hinge head to rotor. why? why do you hate fun?

I am happy to edit this review to positive once these few minor inconveniences are solved.

100$ in DLC and rising. 

Mostly cosmetic...which is..*mostly* fine. 

Except for one thing.. The actual ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game hasnt had an actual update in years.

Its still the same bugged empty ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ nothing game.

Been playing for awhile, and works on windows and steam OS. The game has always had issues with performance so don't expect it to work handheld. I do wish the devs would add some better quality of life stuff though. So often do I find myself relying on workshop mods like "build and repair" to make the game somewhat less dull and playable without constantly backtracking.you really have to know how to play the game ahead of time or play with others as the the "tutorial" does seem to bore anyone that I put towards it. Overall the games community is only supported by the few working workshop mods still working and the load of paid DLC where many useful features are locked behind paywalls.

great game always getting updates to add new and interesting things as well as great workshop mods! all though it could use a little optimization as the 3 or 4 previous game updates have made the game stutter a little at times when there is a lot going on. it is stuttering a little even when set to low in graphics. normally I run the game in the high graphics settings.

In its current state, the base game easily delivers around 400 hours of pure enjoyment. However, the modding community unlocks literally thousands of hours of extra playtime (some people have tens of thousands). I have 2,708 hours so far, and the game still feels as fresh and exciting as day one.

For almost 2 years now on our dedicated server, we run a model(ish!) of the solar system where it takes a ship ~14 real-life days to travel the diameter of the solar system and we so far we have only conquered the Earth, Moon and Mars.

No matter how you want to play this game, there are the community and tools to make it happen.

Very scummy and annoying (at least for a beginner) when half the items in the creative inventory are greyed out to advertise the DLC they're from with no way to turn them off. 

Didn't make me want to buy the DLC at all (or the second game which I was considering buying), made me want to refund the game instead if I hadn't already spent more than 2 hours on it.

I first got my hands on this game when I was little. A friend introduced me to it, and to the whole concept of it, and I understood almost nothing. Building blocks, oxygen, power grids, thrusters, inertia, none of it clicked. I poked at it for a bit, didn't get what I was supposed to be doing, got bored, and wandered off to something simpler.

Then years later I came across it again, and something had changed. Maybe it was me. With a bit more age and patience, all the stuff that used to fly over my head suddenly made sense, and I realized this game is way more intriguing than I ever gave it credit for.

Space Engineers is basically a sandbox that respects your intelligence and your willingness to fail. You mine, you build, you wire up power and conveyors, you slap thrusters on something and pray it doesn't tip over and grind itself into a wall. The physics are the real star. Everything has mass, everything has momentum, and the game is more than happy to let you learn that the hard way when your half finished ship drifts off into the void or folds in on itself the moment you forget a support.

What got me the second time around was the freedom. There's no hand holding telling you the "right" way to do anything. You want a tiny mining pod, build it. You want a massive capital ship that takes three real life hours and a small amount of your sanity, go for it. The satisfaction of watching something you designed from scratch actually fly is hard to beat, and the failures are honestly just as fun in hindsight.

It's not a game that explains itself well, which is exactly why little me bounced off it. It asks for patience and rewards curiosity, and if you give it both, it opens up into something genuinely special. Coming back to it as someone who could finally appreciate what it was doing felt like discovering it for the first time, except this time I actually got it.

⭐ 9 out of 10. Steep at first, brutal with physics, but one of those rare sandboxes that grows with you. Glad I gave it a second chance.

Imean, its real ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ epic.
Bit hard on the start but if u got any doubt hop in any public server and ask away. Real nice people

Space Engineers is a great game for people looking to flex their creativity and burn out on a long grind with results that can make you proud. Good game. Love it. Just keep your builds small if your solo, easy to get carried away. :)
It is way better than the simplified and super unfinished early access sequal.

game has its issues but is a lot of fun if you like building things. can be a bit grindy at first but it gets easy later on. and there are so many mods if you really plan to explore this game its gonna take along time before you run out of content
Good game. Now, if they'd impliment version control for steam workshop? It would be a great game.

This game is amazing.  The building machanics are really fun and you can get imersed for hours.  Does have quite a learning curve though, so early game can feel like a grind if you don't know what you are doing.
An excellent game, with a well developed worshop. I spent too many hours here.
Excellent game, mods can flesh out the empty bits.

I am new to the game. Have been for a while now. Will be a while until I can call myself "decent" at the game.

Do note the game is performance intensive. Try to run it on a computer that isn't prone to overheating, and be prepared to turn down the graphics. It also requires actual, complex thinking to an extent few games do.

Great game for creative individuals that enjoy freedom and no real set of rules or constraints. Challengine to  initiallty learn but well worth every minute. Crazy developer support and huge mod selection from community members. 
Highly recommend
If you are a sanbox gamer who enjoys little to no narrative structure, and a large toolbox to employ, this is it. Scratches the colony/factory/exploration itch just right.
good game but some things need to be modified e.g the sun moving instead of the planets
Very much a sandbox where you can do whatever you want. Lots of mods if you want to change up the vanilla game play or modify some aspect of it.

If you're thinking you'll like this game, don't buy it. You'll get sucked in. I've had this for a week and already put in over sixty hours. 

10/10 would hyperfocus again.

this game is crazy but be prepared to give every hour on this game, no toilit not bathroom no nothing, ive speent hours trying to learn one thing starting off and devoting everything to make a tung tung sahur ship. this game is a better version of trail makers and mincraft. please get this game and join its small comuity.
fun game, takes a bit to learn but very fun once you figure it out, also VERY fun with friends and mods (i did not crash the ship it was your fault)

Well....what can I say? I got finished with Satisfactory a few weeks back (amazing game, one of the greatest games I've ever played), and I decided to give this a try. It's another game that you don't want to stop  playing.....you'll get sucked in when you figure out the mechanics. It's gonna be hard at first because this game does not hold your hand, but once it clicks, you'll be alright.
very good game very laggy with big amounts of mods on lower end computers
bought this to play cross plat with a friend just to find out its only possible through public dedicated servers which defeated the purpose of buying this game
Great sandbox game, lots of unique blocks to build with an the freedom to build where you like /how you like
it is a fun game and has lots of mod support

I'm enjoying the game so far. I'm not such a big fan of the block building style. I much prefer the building system that Icarus uses. However, I'm learning how to get around decently. As I play more I will edit my review.

Wonderful, allows creativity and I'm already starting to regret writing this, my hands hurt after 2h 15m of English Literature. I also dont know how I haven't written a review for this with 981 hours.

9/10 - Great game, DLCs for too much content. In my opinion games should have 5 DLCs at most, to add more content add them to existing DLCs.

It's alright.

Lots of engineering and creativity to be had, especially in multiplayer.
Holy cow, i love this game. I've spent three years waiting on rotors to craft. Thanks Splitsie for the tutorials!!!

It's a really great game, giving you pretty much all the freedom you could want out of a space game, you just need to get past the learning "cliff"(i recommend Splitsies Youtube tutorials). I also really love the survival mechanics, my only issue being it just simply takes too long to do anything in this game. 
Batteries take hours or even a day(irl time) to charge and deplete, and progression is slow paced and grindy. But that's not any reason to dislike this game, as the grindiness is easily tolerable.
EDIT: ok so turns out space engineers is just finicky on linux, so it was just me SO IGNORE EVERYTHING PAST THIS POINT(still recommend a decent pc as this game is kinda hefty, and if youre on linux just be prepared to troubleshoot bad performance)
[strike]The performance issues are too much for me, as i get frequent fps drops and audio stutters with the highest i ever gotten being 30 FPS,
and i can't even change the graphic settings without it crashing, sometimes even just making a world can crash the game.
i have nvidia geforce rtx 5060(even if steam doesnt detect it), this shouldn't be happening[/strike]
I DO recommend this game for anyone who wants space freedom and can deal with the slow paced survival mode and learning cliff, and maybe potential performance issues but that may just be me.
If I NEED to build a spaceship (Which is a thing that happens sometimes...) This is the game! 10/10
super steep learning curve but overall super fun, endless hours of creativity 10/10 recomend

In my entire 148 hours of playing this game, I have never once flew a spaceship of my own making (that wasn't the spawn ship)... 10/10 game, The dramatic ship crashes are funny, one minute you're driving your respawn truck calmly, the next, you're upside down. Deserves Game of the Year.

This game is not for everyone, and the grind in the early game can get nasty if you're unlucky.

BUT, it is a blast to mess around with your friends, and every hardship is only a reason for you to build something amazing. If you can get past the early game, you're set for a blast!

(Just keep in mind big stuff can make stuff laggy on lower-end systems)

Everything I wanted out of Starfield, and more. Bit hard to figure out the early stages, but now I'm obsessed. Best survival space game I've played, no contest. Downsides are the price of the DLCs, but it's a small studio so cut them some lack, okay? It's still better than most AAA games from the last five years.
its like dayz but like not dayz but like is dayz? idk man all i know is its dayz in space but space ship is dayzombi but not dayz cause make ship but dayz cause gun
Best game ever, its sandbox its hard (that is good), its just perfect in every way.

i absolutely ADORE this game, its kinda difficult to learn at first; but its amazing for people who have a lot of creativity.
in short: heres what you're in for in this game.
this game is a minecraft-stormworks like game but with a spin of space. it has way more content than other games like stormworks even in its beta phases. the only part i dont like is that a lot of the decorations are locked behind 5$ DLC's.
Hands down one of the best games ever made by any game company anywhere.. Space Engineers and Keen have very few equals.

Enter new survival scenario
flip rover and gets stuck on mountain
proceed to starve to death, more time I would like to admit
spend 2 hours flipping rover down the mountain.
10/10 would recommend. A very cathartic experience.
I don't like coding at all but this game is really fun and it really opens up to me abit, space engineers kinda reminds me of no mans sky, but i will continue to play :)

Works fine on Ubuntu LTS Vanilla steam, Vanilla Proton.
Son loves it, we play together.
Please add SteamOS support logo, and keep it working on Proton!

Introduction:
For me this game is like having a way to play with space ships both in a playful way and in a serious way is the perfect balance between simulation and toy like behavior. This game of course by itself if fun but it lacks goals so if you need goals to have fun then this sandbox is not for you, but remember the huge part of what makes this game better overtime is the mod community and the servers, similar on what Minecraft offers servers can be a different experience by itself.

The Vanilla Experience:
This game offers good vanilla experience but is not great to have many hours of play, you definably need Vanilla + or fully moded game to bring the best of it.

The Moded Dedicated Server with Friend Experience:

This is for me the best thing that I played with friends is a full KSP like adventure with friends and with mods and plugins the game gets so much entertaining and is a world where is always evolving and our crazy ideas get built and destroyed over and over again. 

About the DLCs:
Is super annoying that they always are being shown on when selecting blocks and reminding you that you don't have it, so much you end up buying some just for that, is a great way to sell a bad practice in UX in my opinion.

Buy it to play it alone or with friends is a very good game.
Ya pretty fun game especially with friends but if your friend lands on your planet your done...

I have 20 hours here from just a few days, and 600+ elsewhere.
This game is incredible, but very difficult to learn. Find a friend who can teach you the ropes, or look up guides either on Youtube or in the game itself. If you like creative games, it will be worth the effort.
It can run on surprisingly weak hardware, I can do multiplayer and singleplayer on low settings and 2G wi-fi.
Small Studio. Great game. Honestly one of my favourite games physics to funny moments anything it all was fun along the journey although hard best game ive played in the space genre.

Its space and creations from planet to moon to deep space, with mods you never know what you'll get, and even more so with updates here and there and fixes when things come up why not join the space race again.
Fantastic open, fully buildable, fully destroyable, and loads of time killing things to do! So much fun.

if you like having to go to google every few minutes, then this game is for you. Even the tutorial does little to help, and hardly anything is explained well enough. You'd be better off playing No Man's Sky

I really wanted to like this game, and I can absolutely see the scope and complexity that will make it a dream for many people. Unfortunately I found the learning curve too steep and the controls/interface too unintuitive that I gave up.

Side note: showing me all the dlc blocks was completely overwhelming and pointless. I would have to have been much further in the game to consider dlc

$43 dollars of cosmetic DLC.
one of my favorite things about the game is the workshop ships so when the cosmetic dlc adds things that are very useful for building ships they are going to get used so a lot of workshop ships require some dlc but it just annoying

I get that this is just meant to be a sandbox, but even so it does stretch the realms of pointlessness a bit. I  got to the point where I could have built a ship to go into space but by then I could just not be bothered to actually do it.

I used to play Empyrion, which I quit because there were so many issues with the implementation. SE is better built, but much less of a game underneath it. Maybe its just not aimed at me.
Does not have a new player friendly tutorial. The tutorial explains very little as to how to operate the controls and interfaces.

THIS GAME SUCKS... No instructions for both single player or open world, this has potential but currently just sucks as is, you cant expect to know what to do with no tutorial, and be thrown in to either single player mode or multiplayer and have no instructions as well as poor player communications, NO ONE wants to help you if your new, and they just ignore you. BUT over all other than the complaint the PC definitely runs smoothly on my set up, I have attached my PC specs below... I'm hoping in the future this game is better but as of now I'm refunding it and finding a different game, because this game isn't worth the stress or headaches.

Anyways, I had a blast playing this game with online coop to boot, what more is there to ask for!? Driving a space ♥♥♥♥ at the speed of sound and then crash landing on a laien planet was raelly quite the exhilirating experimence... I cant wait to hop back on and go again and discover strange new worlds to boldy go where no man has go before...

not nearly good enough to be this overpriced but sadly this is the only game like it so you have to contend with the jank. everything is place individual then very those individual have to manual "build" to make it function

Has no tutorial and what little in-game advice is provided doesn't really help you to understand the game. Super confusing,
Get Satisfactory instead. Better game, better everything

Never have I ever given a bad review to a game.

Well, today's the day.

The tutorial kept on soft-locking me, or whatever it is, "Quick-start". Couldn't understand how to destroy stuff for the first 10 minutes. The trailer was SO different from this "garbage".

I was expecting something like trailmakers + no man's sky + stellaris. This was so much worse.

I couldn't figure out how to move building blocks, because the tutorial was terrible. I couldn't... for the life of me, figure out how I was so excited about this before I bought it.

Building makes me want to refund the game, which I most probably will! 90% of the game is behind two thousand DLCs. I bought three, and I only unlocked like, 13 building blocks or, whatever the hell.

3/10, I do NOT recommend this game.
Probably the best looking, most potential having piece of absolut ♥♥♥♥ i have ever seen.
good stuff for kamukze tactics, 10 out of 10 would bomb myself again

I didn't realize they added fido's ancestral cousins and got the ♥♥♥♥ scared out of me by some very hungry puppers. 10 raw mammal meat out of 10 would hide inside of my starter rover until morning again.
It's a consistent favourite. It has an emphasis on building, but alsoi has plenty of survival elements, wrapped up in a space theme.
it is very fun to mess around in sandbox and create your own star ships
I have 75 hours and still enjoy creating ships and bases. The building system and physics are great, and there is always something new to try

-start survival
-get resources
-build mining ship-get base
-starts larger projects
-gets clanged
-10/10
its a great game it also scratches that space urge if you love space

I purchased Space Engineers in 2022 for $10, tried it out but never put the time in. I have been following the updates and watching youtubeineers over the years. The past few updates looked really good, but I jumped back in with the latest Economy II update.  Man, I was sleeping on this!  

At around 60 hours in, experimenting and going back and forth to youtube tutorials (I speak English, but I needed a translator ;) It "started" to make sense. Like Jethro Clampett "I think I finally figured this game out!"

Overall fantastic investment from my past life! Just picked up some DLC's 50% off for reasons.

Let's see if this monkey goes to heaven!

Well you have to be a engineer. I built a toaster, some may say you need to build a space ship, i say you build a lazer burning toast maker. You can build anything you want. fly on a planet, fly out of the planet, fight people on the planet, drill into the planet, collect resources, put guns on your toaster, and make your enemys toast whilst toasting some toast. Its a learning curve. Theres diffrenet levels to this, and you arent on mine
Literally trash. Everyone who things otherwise, is nothing short of insane.
at the beginning of the tutorial a wolf attacked and killed me, before I learned how to kill wildlife.. trash game...
build ship, lose ship, build another ship, lose the other ship, rinse and repete
Great game. Scratches that nomadic ship survival itch that few games do.

Keen's dedication to regular updates, and a thriving mod community and in-game community takes this  sandbox from 'pleasant' to 'vastly engrossing'.

Digital legos for the creative types, with solid space-survival mechanics, resources, and physics (and many great mods) for the survivalists, good community for RPers... Lots to delve into, fairly high skill ceiling with a steep initial learning curve (look to YouTube to help learn the basics and get inspired, like I did!). Easy to spend many, many hours designing ships, flying and driving, engaging in high-stakes ship-to-ship combat, and testing yourself with survival scenarios.
Just be creative and the game is not limited to any rules

This is one where the neutral button would be useful.

Very, VERY flexible sandbox for building and crafting, but the menus are a hot mess. Like iredeemably bad. They feel like they've been made as a placeholder during some Proof of concept or early alpha and then never been properly iterated on.
They are messy, unintuitive, completely hairbrained in their layout, have way too much uselsess information while also not providing a lot of relevant info unless you know in exactly which submenu of a submenu you need to look and the ingame tutorial basically tells you to google it. (There are some links to some videos but they are outdated AF)

Then there's the wolves. Or Ballistic ordinance with teeth.
They spawn 20km away, smell you, pick the straightest line posible right vor your crotch and start biting. The amount of times i've tabbed out to read an email or google something real quick and come back to my corpse next to a wolf is mindblowing. and let's not even talk about their sad excuse for weapons. they handle, sound and feel like toys. all of them.

But, the crafting and building are excelent. If you enjoy a deep and complex building system and don't mind having 2 wikis, 4 youtube videos and 3 reddit threads open while you play then this is for you.

As a full time engineer I love being able to come home and maximise my depression. 

10/10 would recomend.
best games since minecraft tauches my tism in just the write ways
control are little bit to much but it a good game
I honestly cannot believe they still update this. Now with 73% less clang.

Very difficult for new players. In-game guidance is almost non-existent. For the little that's there it's often buggy and  randomly appears and disappears. Ended up having to gather from many sources for a basic starter tutorial (one good tutorial for PC is https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TZxEVe2H_Gs&list=PLfMGCUepUcNy2XHRLrGo0Dqsg86E7D3og by Splitsie).

Then again, by following the above tutorial in the quick start scenario, it takes an hour of stone mining just to get the basic refinery and assembler going. Maybe the sandbox mode is the way to go with the game.

Unlike most space games that revolve around pre build ships the thing i like most about this one is that you can make anything as big as complicated or as simple as you want and it still can function and fly!
